claimText: The Coal Controller's Organization (CCO) acts as the appellate authority for disputes between consumers and owners regarding the declaration of coal grade and size.
evidenceExcerpt: CCO acts as the appellate authority for disputes between consumers and owners regarding the declaration of coal grade and size.
examUse: Statement-based question on the statutory functions and dispute resolution mechanisms of the CCO.
distractorHooks: Attributing this appellate role to Coal India Limited (CIL)
Attributing this role to the Ministry of Coal directly

claimText: Coal gasification produces syngas, which is a mixture consisting of carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen (H2), carbon dioxide (CO2), and methane (CH4).
evidenceExcerpt: Syngas is a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen (H2), carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4), and other gases.
examUse: Statement-based or multiple-choice question identifying the constituent gases of syngas.
distractorHooks: Including sulfur dioxide (SO2) or nitrogen oxides (NOx) as primary components

claimText: The Coal Gasification Financial Incentive Scheme (CGFIS) aims to achieve 100 million tonnes of coal gasification by the year 2030.
evidenceExcerpt: The Coal Gasification Financial Incentive Scheme (CGFIS) aims to achieve 100 million tonnes of coal gasification by 2030.
examUse: Statement-based question testing factual knowledge of government energy transition targets.
distractorHooks: Changing the target year to 2050 or 2070
Changing the target volume to 50 or 500 million tonnes
